text: Fix backend test harness: vitest-based suites fail under node --test runner
description: packages/backend `npm test` (NODE_ENV=test node scripts/run-tests.mjs) runs the suites through node --test/tsx, but src/*.test.ts import from 'vitest' (db, server, forces, embeddings, value-calculation). Result: 5/6 files fail with "Vitest failed to find the runner" — independent of any source change (reproduces at HEAD). Either run these via `vitest` (add vitest config + script) or migrate the imports to node:test (as shared/ already uses). Until fixed, verify backend logic via `tsx --test` per-suite + `tsc --noEmit`. Discovered 2026-05-31 while landing the connection contribution-% feature.

reflections.context: Backend `npm test` failed (5/6 files) because run-tests.mjs invoked tsx --test (node:test) on files that import from vitest.
reflections.outcome: Pointed the script at `vitest run` (vitest was already hoisted at root and passing), declared vitest as a backend devDep, and deleted the dead runner. npm test -w backend now passes 31/31.
reflections.effectiveness: Minimal, correct fix — the tests were never broken, only the runner was wrong. Declaring the dep removes reliance on accidental hoisting.
reflections.lesson: When a wrapper script looks broken, check whether the tool is merely invoked the wrong way before assuming the tests themselves are at fault. Also: this aim sat at priority -1.0 purely from value-graph disconnection, not low worth — a reminder that priority misranks infra/tooling lacking an intrinsic-value ancestor.
reflections.pattern: Same diagnosis-before-rewrite approach as the connection-feature verification; small infra fixes often just need the right invocation.

status.comment: Fixed: backend `test` script now runs `NODE_ENV=test vitest run` (was tsx --test via run-tests.mjs); declared vitest as a backend devDependency (^4.1.8, previously only hoisted at root); removed the orphaned scripts/run-tests.mjs. Verified: npm test -w backend → 6 files / 31 tests pass. Added test:watch too.
